title: 4-Vinylcyclohexene (4-VCH) - Canada.ca

## 4-Vinylcyclohexene (4-VCH)
- 4 -Vinylcyclohexene (4-VCH)
This substance was identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP).
- Summary of publications
- Follow-up activities
- Information gathering Section 71 CMP 2023
Draft screening assessment
Conclusion on section 64 criteria
Follow-up activities
4-Vinylcyclohexene (4-VCH)
Cyclohexene, 4-ethenyl-
Information gathering

## Follow-up activities
While exposure of the general population to 4-VCH is not of concern at current levels, this substance is considered to have a health effect of concern on the basis of carcinogenicity. Therefore, there may be a concern for human health if exposures were to increase. Follow-up activities may involve including the substance in future information gathering initiatives, such as a mandatory survey under section 71 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 (CEPA 1999). Stakeholders would be given advance notice of any planned information gathering.

## Information gathering
In June 2023, a notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 157, No. 25 – June 24, 2023 under section 71 of CEPA 1999. This notice applies to 4-VCH (CAS RN 100-40-3). More information is available on the Information gathering with respect to certain substances under the Chemicals Management Plan web page.
Publication in Canada Gazette , Part I, of a notice under section 71 of CEPA 19.
Publication of the final screening assessment. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 152, No. 10 .
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the draft screening assessment (including follow-up activities). The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 151, No. 8 .
